prompt: Using RFdiffusion, design a de novo helical bundle protein (50–70 residues) that competitively blocks the receptor-binding site of hemagglutinin H3 (Influenza A H3N2) without requiring antibody framework scaffolds. The designed protein should achieve nanomolar affinity for HA trimers. outline a methodology for: (1) RFdiffusion backbone generation targeting the HA receptor-binding pocket, (2) sequence design using ProteinMPNN, (3) computational affinity prediction and off-target specificity assessment. Include discussion of challenges from HA antigenic drift.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: Hemagglutinin H3; organism: Influenza A virus (H3N2); catalogue version v1.0
